name: Springerle (Molded Christmas Cookies)
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat eggs until very light and fluffy. Gradually add sugar; beat for 15 minutes. Do not underbeat.
2. Fold in anise extract and flour. Roll dough to a thickness of 3/8 inch.
3. Thoroughly flour the springerle mold or rolling pin. Press molds firmly into the dough.
4. Cut cookies apart and place on a greased and floured cookie sheet.
5. Let dry overnight at room temperature, covered with paper towels or uncovered.
6. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
7. Place cookies in the oven and immediately reduce the temperature to 300°F (150°C).
8. Bake for 15 minutes. Cookies should not brown.
9. Store cookies for 2 to 3 weeks to allow the flavor to mellow. These cookies are very hard and may be used for dunking in coffee, tea, or cocoa.
10. Paint designs with egg yolk colored with food coloring (optional).
